PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
WHAT ’ S THE REGISTRATION TIMELINE?
The best day and time to schedule your webinar depends on:
• 	What your market has been trained to expect
• 	Whether or not they can attend during standard working hours
• 	What time zone they live in
• 	What’s currently popular
If your promotion is good enough and your prospects feel a strong
attachment to you, you can do it anytime and still have success.
But in general, the best times are: 4 pm ET / 9 pm ET / 10 am ET and in
general, the best days are: Wednesday, Thursday and Sunday.
Ideally, you want to run 3 live broadcasts of your webinar. Here are two
suggested schedules:
✓ 	4 pm ET Wednesday
✓ 	9 pm ET Wednesday
✓ 	4 pm ET Thursday
✓ 	4 pm ET Wednesday
✓ 	4 pm ET Thursday
✓ 	9 pm ET Thursday
What will your registration timeline be?
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
______________________________________________________________________________
Once you’ve opened up registration, it should remain open for 3-5 days;
ideally 3 days.
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	3 	19
bonus tip!
don’t use savings and standard times but do standardize to
eastern time zone and communicate major time zones

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
HOW CAN I OPTIMIZE MY WEBINAR
REGISTRATION PAGE (AND WRITE A
GREAT HEADLINE)?
When creating your webinar registration page:
✓ 	It must be a stand-alone page
✓ 	There should be no navigation (other than privacy/terms of service) or
links taking them off the page (they either register, or they leave)
✓ 	You should test registering for your webinar from your page
And when it comes to the elements on your webinar registration page,
simple is best. Here’s all you need:
✓ 	Headline
✓ 	Webinar details
1-2 sentence description of the webinar
3-5 bullet points (what you’ll discover in this webinar)
✓ 	Date/time of the webinar
✓ 	Signup button
✓ 	Language that clearly indicates that they’re registering for a free webinar
✓ 	Privacy notice
Let’s look at your headline, your webinar details, and testing in more detail…
HEADLINE
You need your headline to:
✓ 	Get them to read the rest of the webinar description
✓ 	Pique their interest
✓ 	Begin to describe what’s in the webinar
✓ 	Dangle the hook for the webinar
When writing your headline:
✓ 	Keep it simple
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	4 	19
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 19 --

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
✓ 	Draw people in with a question, a statement that invites consideration, a
controversy, or a promise
✓ 	Be consistent with the type of content you will be teaching (a great
headline that doesn’t accurately describe your webinar is a bad headline)
✓ 	Use bold, italics, caps and color to emphasize key concepts in your
headline
✓ 	Remember your headline Pre and Post-Heads: Pre-heads go before the
headline, usually to give context or ask a question, post-heads go after the
headline to deepen the communication or make it apply to them
See the Writing a Great Headline Exercise for a detailed exercise on this topic.
What’s your headline?

WEBINAR REGISTRATION DETAILS
Webinar registration details:
✓ 	1-2 sentence description of the webinar
✓ 	3-5 bullet points (what you’ll discover in this webinar)
When Writing Your Webinar Registration Details
✓ 	Avoid using the word “learn," instead use “discover” or “you’ll have created/
outlined/chosen…”
✓ 	It’s o.k. to go a little long. You’re selling a dream… attendance at your
webinar will be the solution to their problems
✓ 	Use magic words:
✓ 	“By the end of this 90-minute webinar you’ll…”
✓ 	“I’ll show you exactly (or step-by-step) how to…”
✓ 	“In it you’ll…”
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	5 	19

✓ 	Use “so” in each bullet point to focus your webinar on what’s in it for
them

TESTING YOUR REGISTRATION PAGE
You should absolutely test this page:
• 	If you’re going to run the webinar multiple times over time
• 	If you’re going to send ad traffic to it
• 	If you’re going to turn your webinar into an evergreen webinar
It does require traffic but at the very least, you want to test your headline.

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
How to Test
✓ 	Follow instructions on your web page / landing page provider
✓ 	Create a page, duplicate it, make changes to the new page, then publish
both
✓ 	The program will randomly choose which page to show to visitors until it
finds a winner
Testing Priorities
1. 	Headline
2. 	Graphic design of the page
3. 	Language on the button
4. Privacy policy statement
5. 	Registration gift
6. 	Testimonials
What are you going to test?

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
WHAT ARE THE ERRORS TO AVOID ON
MY REGISTRATION PAGE?
Below are the 9 common webinar registration page errors to avoid (use
this as a tool to evaluate your page).
ERROS TO AVOID ON YOUR REGISTRATION PAGE
✓ 	Not designed for mobile
✓ 	Sign up button below the fold
✓ 	Sign up button disappears into the design of the page
✓ 	The page is too complex
✓ 	Not telling them this is a free webinar
✓ 	Too many choices
✓ 	Too many form fields to complete
✓ 	Not running analytics
✓ 	Not including tracking pixels
These are some additional errors to avoid on your webinar registration page:
✓ 	Adding testimonials to your registration page
✓ 	Adding an About-You section
See the Webinar Registration Page Errors To Avoid PDF for more on this topic.

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
WHAT IS A REGISTRATION GIF T AND
HOW DO I BEST USE IT ?
Offering a registration gift is a great way to increase signups for your
webinar. Choose one that:
✓ 	Your prospects really want
✓ 	Contributes to the transformation they desire
✓ 	Moves them towards the mindset they need to be in to purchase on the
webinar
PROMOTING YOUR REGISTRATION GIFT
✓ 	Below the sign up button
✓ 	Usually below the fold
✓ 	It usually helps to have an image of the registration gift and 1-2 sentences
describing it
✓ 	Be sure to emphasize it in your emails promoting registration for the
webinar
✓ 	You may want to consider running an ad offering it (just make it clear that
it’s a gift for registering for the webinar)
Are you going to use a registration gift? If so, what will it be?

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
WHAT ARE THE 3 AUDIENCES?
When it comes to marketing your webinar, your tactics depend on one thing:
whether or not you have your prospects email addresses.
And you basically have 3 potential audiences:
1. 	Internal Traffic: You have their email address
2. 	External Traffic: You don’t have their email address
3. 	Affiliate Traffic: You don’t have their email address but your affiliate does
You want to send all 3 audiences to webinar registration page, but the vast
majority (70%+) won’t sign up for a webinar, so how do you get the email
address from External and Affiliate traffic (because your marketing to them is
much easier and cheaper once you get that email address)? This is how:
AUDIENCE #1: YOU HAVE THEIR EMAIL ADDRESS

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
HOW DO I BEST PROMOTE MY
REGISTRATION PAGE?
There are several ways to promote your webinar registration page:
✓ 	A pre-registration email campaign
✓ 	Driving traffic to your registration page
✓ 	Advertising
✓ 	Additional strategies to test
PRE-REGISTRATION EMAIL STRATEGY
Ideally you want someone on your list before you invite them to register
for a webinar. The best pre-registration strategy starts a week before you
open up registration. Once you announce the webinar, remember that you’re
not talking about your product, you’re talking about why they should attend
the webinar. As people register, remove them out of the pre-registration
promotional sequence (because they’ll be receiving the attendance promo).
✓ 	Part 1: The Build-Up
This enables you to do a lot of the psychology to get them on the webinar
(separately from the webinar registration itself). This would be a series of
emails (2-3), sent the week before registration opens, in which you set up the
problem the webinar will address and start talking about solutions to that
problem, all without ever mentioning the webinar. You want to
communicate:
“You have an issue, it’s affecting you, you’ve not been able to solve it, but there
is a solution out there that will work for you. I know what that is and I’m happy
to share it.”
✓ 	Part 2: Announce The Webinar
This becomes a natural extension of the build-up. You want to communicate:
“Over the last week I’ve sent out several emails talking about_____ and how to
solve it. From the emails and feedback I’ve received, it’s clear that this is an
issue on many people’s minds, I suspect you’re one of them. So, I’ve decided
to put together a webinar on the subject.”
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	12 	19
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 19 --

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
✓ 	PART 3: Promote The Webinar
This would be a series of emails, each of which tells them the name of the
webinar, the days/times of broadcast, and gives them a registration link.
✓ 	Email 1
• 	Send: Day 1 of webinar registration period
• 	Focus: Announce the webinar (1 sentence of what it will be about,
what it will help them achieve)
• 	Include: 3 live broadcast times
✓ 	Email 2
• 	Send: Day 3 of webinar registration period
• 	Focus: The benefits of attending the webinar (how their life will be
better after attending...often this is story-based, showing someone’s
life before and after transformation)
• 	Include: Registration gift and why it’s valuable
✓ 	Email 3
• 	Send: Morning of the webinar
• 	Focus: “Last chance to register… Just finishing up the script, and
here’s something I just added” and talk about what they will get
and how this will affect their life
• 	Include: Countdown timer to first broadcast
✓ 	Email 4
• 	Send: 15-30 minutes before first performance
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	13 	19
remember!
during the pre-registration period, you’re not talking
about your product, you’re talking about why they
should attend the webinar
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
• 	Focus: “We’re starting now” and brief summary of what they’ll get
out of it.
• 	Include: Reminder of registration gift
✓ 	Email 5
• 	Send: After first webinar, before the second
• 	Focus: “It was amazing! Here’s what we covered, here are just a few
of the comments people made about it. There are two more live
broadcasts (yes, they will be live and I will be answering your
questions).”
• 	Include: Last chance to get registration gift
✓ 	Email 6
• 	Send: After the second, before the third
• 	Focus: “Last chance to attend live. Here are some of the things
people have loved… And I’ll be answering your questions live.”
• 	Include: More testimonials (this email often focuses on a single
great testimonial, with the other testimonials thrown into the p.s.)

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
DRIVING TRAFFIC TO YOUR REGISTRATION PAGE
How you drive traffic to your registration page will depend on whether or not
you own the traffic.
Traffic You Own
✓ 	Drive people to your registration page via traffic you own, for example via:
• 	Emails to your list (note, that’s plural)
• 	Blog posts
• 	Every visitor to your site/blog should see a promo for your webinar (an
Ad, a Popup, top/bottom bars)
• 	Podcast
• 	Push notifications
• 	Text notifications
• 	Physical mailing
• 	Just talking with people
• 	Calling people
• 	Social media (including group posts)
• 	Live broadcasts
• 	Messenger posts
Traffic You Don’t Own
✓ 	Drive people to your registration page via traffic you don’t own, for
example via:
• 	Affiliate mailings
• 	Posts on other people’s social media groups and forums
• 	Paid advertising
Notes:

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
ADVERTISING YOUR REGISTRATION PAGE
Advertising can be very effective. But it depends on: your attendance rate,
your conversion rate, and your price. The key is profitability. We recommend
that you not use paid advertising until your webinar is converting at 10% or
higher (feel free to spend a bit on advertising for your next webinar, but don’t
try to build out an entire paid webinar funnel until you’ve got a proven,
converting webinar). That said, breakeven traffic still builds your list and
creates positive attitudes. The key is to measure… Don’t measure cost per
registrant or cost per attendee. Instead, measure cost per conversion.
Ad Strategy 1: Direct to Webinar Registration
Ad Strategy 2: Optin Incentive to Webinar Registration
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	16 	19
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

ADDITIONAL STRATEGIES TO TEST
Here are 3 additional strategies to test when thinking about promoting your
webinar registration page:
✓ 	The Bare Page
• 	Lets the ad (or email for people already on your list) do the selling
• 	Only has 4 elements
• 	Image that illustrates the benefit
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	17 	19

or higher (feel free to spend a bit on advertising for

-- 17 of 19 --

PLANNER / M2. WEBINAR REGISTRATION PLANNER
• 	Solution based headline “lose 5 pounds in 5 days, guaranteed”
• 	Countdown timer
• 	Registration button
✓ 	The Optin Incentive to Webinar Registration Funnel
• 	Specifically design for ad-based cold traffic
• 	Gives before asking for a commitment
• 	Captures email address before webinar registration (remember, 70%
won’t sign up for a webinar)
• 	The funnel includes:
• 	Ad featuring optin offer leading to…
• 	Optin offer leading to…
• 	Webinar registration page (webinar registration is optin thank you
page)
• 	Keys to success
• 	Compelling optin offer (e.g. a report)
• 	Webinar is logical continuation of the optin offer (builds on what
they were just promised)
• 	Webinar registration page acknowledges that fact and gives logical
reason why they should attend (“Show you step-by-step, exactly
how to…”, “Coach you, answering your specific questions”)
• 	It’s important to explain how the webinar will give them even more
than what they’ll get in the report
• 	Often a video is needed to connect the logic of why a webinar is
needed/advantageous
✓ 	Adding an Exit Pop-Over to your Registration Page
Consider adding an exit pop-over to your registration page. In other words, as
they leave (and didn’t register for your webinar), give them the opportunity to
receive something cool, (other than the registration bonus).
To do this, you need two separate versions of your webinar registration page,
one with an exit pop-up, one without (don’t run an exit pop up on traffic that
comes from an ad).
© Internet Alchemy, Inc. 	of	18 	19
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

and prepares them for the rest of the webinar funnel.
